step_by_step_reasoning: Drone flights captured by licensed pilots upload images to a third-party processing system. The automated AWS workflow starts with a scheduled CloudWatch Event triggering Step Functions. The Step Functions orchestrate three separate Lambda functions sequentially: Lambda 1 checks the third-party API for new images; Lambda 2 transfers those images from the third-party system to Amazon S3; Lambda 3 creates a raster proxy URL for the images and stores it in the on-premises database. Maintenance and operation teams use on-premises mapping software, which reads the proxy database and retrieves the actual high-resolution images from S3 via AWS Storage Gateway.

graph.name: Marathon Oil Serverless Drone Imagery Pipeline
graph.notes: Automated pipeline transferring drone aerial photos to local GIS systems without modifying legacy on-prem software, leveraging AWS Storage Gateway and Step Functions orchestration.

nodes.name: Drone Image Host
nodes.notes: Third-party platform hosting processed ortho mosaics and elevation models.

nodes.name: CloudWatch Events
nodes.notes: Cron-style trigger to initiate the workflow scheduler.

nodes.name: Orchestrator
nodes.notes: Coordinates the multi-phase execution of the Lambda functions.

nodes.name: Check Flights Lambda
nodes.notes: Queries the third-party software API to identify new imagery flights.

nodes.name: Ingestion Lambda
nodes.notes: Pulls identified drone images from the third party and writes them to S3.

nodes.name: Raster Proxy Lambda
nodes.notes: Generates proxy links mapping the on-premises server to S3.

nodes.name: Drone Imagery Bucket
nodes.notes: Stores high-resolution images, digital terrain, and digital elevation models.

nodes.name: Mapping Server
nodes.notes: On-premises mapping software that queries local DB and displays images.

nodes.name: On-Premises Database
nodes.notes: Stores index metadata and raster proxy URL links.

nodes.name: File Gateway
nodes.notes: Exposes S3 buckets to the local mapping server as a network file share.

nodes.name: Maintenance & Ops Teams
nodes.notes: Internal users examining imagery for well pad equipment monitoring.

edges.notes: Checks API for new drone flights
Inserts raster proxy URL mapping details
Views well pad imagery via map client
Requests file paths through mounted gateway
Translates file system request to S3 GET
